commands_run:
node --import tsx/esm -e "import React, { useRef } from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useClipDrag } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.Element = dom.window.Element; globalThis.Node = dom.window.Node; globalThis.PointerEvent = dom.window.MouseEvent; const applyCalls = []; const selectClipCalls = []; const selectClipsCalls = []; const coordinator = { update: () => ({ time: 1, rowIndex: 0, trackId: 'V1', trackKind: 'visual', trackName: 'V1', isNewTrack: false, isReject: false, newTrackKind: null, screenCoords: { rowTop: 0, rowLeft: 0, rowWidth: 400, rowHeight: 48, clipLeft: 100, clipWidth: 100, ghostCenter: 150 } }), end: () => undefined, get lastPosition(){ return null; } }; function App(){ const timelineWrapperRef = useRef(null); const dataRef = useRef({ rows: [{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 0, end: 1, effectId: 'effect-1' }] }, { id: 'V2', actions: [{ id: 'clip-2', start: 2, end: 3, effectId: 'effect-2' }] }], tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }, { id: 'V2', kind: 'visual', label: 'V2' }], meta: { 'clip-1': { track: 'V1' }, 'clip-2': { track: 'V2' } }, clipOrder: { V1: ['clip-1'], V2: ['clip-2'] } }); useClipDrag({ timelineWrapperRef, dataRef, moveClipToRow: (...args) => applyCalls.push({ kind: 'singleMove', args }), createTrackAndMoveClip: (...args) => applyCalls.push({ kind: 'newTrackMove', args }), applyTimelineEdit: (rows) => applyCalls.push({ kind: 'apply', rows }), selectClip: (clipId, opts) => selectClipCalls.push({ clipId, toggle: Boolean(opts?.toggle) }), selectClips: (clipIds) => selectClipsCalls.push([...clipIds]), selectedClipIdsRef: { current: new Set(['clip-1', 'clip-2']) }, coordinator, rowHeight: 48, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, startLeft: 0 }); return React.createElement('div', { ref: timelineWrapperRef }, React.createElement('button', { className: 'clip-action', 'data-clip-id': 'clip-1', 'data-row-id': 'V1' }, 'clip-1')); } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); const clip = document.querySelector('.clip-action'); await act(async () => { clip.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerdown', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 10, clientY: 10 })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointermove', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 30, clientY: 10 })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerup', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 30, clientY: 10 })); }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ applyCalls, selectClipCalls, selectClipsCalls })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

task_updates.executor_notes: Wired the multi-drag pointer-up path to compute a shared timeDelta from the anchor clip, derive newStart and newEnd for every clip in clipOffsets, resolve the group placement through resolveGroupOverlaps, and commit one atomic applyTimelineEdit update while preserving the dragged multi-selection via selectClips. Verification showed the multi-drag path issued a single applyTimelineEdit call that moved both clips together and preserved the full dragged selection instead of collapsing to the anchor clip.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified with a jsdom useClipDrag probe that a multi-drag drop produced one applyTimelineEdit call moving both selected clips and then emitted selectClips(['clip-1','clip-2']) with no fallback selectClip call, so the multi-selection remains intact after drop instead of collapsing to the anchor clip.
